name: Truck Parham
abstract: Charles Valdez "Truck" Parham (January 25, 1911 – June 5, 2002) was an American jazz double-bassist. Parham was born in Chicago and was first a professional sportsman: he was a boxer and played football with the Chicago Negro All Stars.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Goin' to Chicago

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: Truck Parham, who at that time... They helped me. They taught me.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Scoops Carey and I think it was Truck Parham was the bass player... we played in the Three Deecees in Chicago with Art Tatum.

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: he said, 'Come by the house' ... and he showed me how he played double stops. ... he wanted to help me -- a young kid
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Theodore Saunders
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ... Truck Parham... [INTERVIEWEE] Right, well, I was instrumental in that.

about_quotes.speaker: Quinn Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I met Truck. I can't remember exactly when I first met Truck.
